# svelte-tel-input

> A lightweight Svelte 5 telephone input component with international phone number formatting, parsing, and validation. Powered by libphonenumber-js.

- Requires Svelte 5 (uses runes: `$state`, `$derived`, `$effect`)
- Install: `pnpm add svelte-tel-input`
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/svelte-tel-input
- Source: https://github.com/gyurielf/svelte-tel-input

## Core concept

`value` is always stored in **E.164 format** (e.g. `+36301234567`). The component handles display formatting — the consumer only ever stores and reads E.164.

## Minimal usage

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  import { TelInput } from 'svelte-tel-input';
  import type { CountryCode } from 'svelte-tel-input/types';

  let country: CountryCode | null = $state('US');
  let value = $state('');
  let valid = $state(true);
</script>

<TelInput bind:country bind:value bind:valid />
```

## Key props (all optional except the bindings you need)

| Prop | Type | Default | Bindable | Notes |
|------|------|---------|----------|-------|
| `value` | `string` | `''` | Yes | E.164 phone number. Source of truth. |
| `country` | `CountryCode \| null` | `null` | Yes | ISO 3166 Alpha-2. Auto-detected from `value` when not set. |
| `valid` | `boolean` | `true` | Yes | Overall validity. |
| `validationError` | `ValidationError` | `null` | Yes | Granular error reason. |
| `detailedValue` | `DetailedValue \| null` | `null` | Yes | Full parsed result (all formats, national number, URI, …). |
| `el` | `HTMLInputElement` | — | Yes | Ref to the underlying `<input>`. |
| `defaultCountry` | `CountryCode \| null` | `null` | No | Country restored by `api.reset()`. |
| `initialFormat` | `'international' \| 'national'` | `'international'` | No | Display format on load and on external value changes. `'national'` hides the dial code in the input; `value` stays E.164. |
| `options` | `TelInputOptions` | `{}` | No | See options below. |
| `id`, `name`, `class`, `disabled`, `readonly`, `required`, `placeholder`, `autocomplete` | standard HTML | | No | Forwarded to the inner `<input type="tel">`. |

## Options (`TelInputOptions`)

```typescript
interface TelInputOptions {
  autoPlaceholder?: boolean;          // default: true  — country-specific placeholder
  spaces?: boolean;                   // default: true  — show "215 456 7890" vs "2154567890"
  validateOn?: 'input' | 'blur' | 'always';  // default: 'always'
  allowedCountries?: CountryCode[];   // mark other countries invalid
  lockCountry?: boolean;              // default: false — prevent country auto-switch
}
```

## Callback props (all optional)

```typescript
onValueChange?: (value: string, details: DetailedValue | null) => void;
onCountryChange?: (country: CountryCode | null) => void;  // fires on auto-detection only
onValidityChange?: (valid: boolean, error: ValidationError) => void;
onError?: (error: string) => void;
onLoad?: () => void;
```

## Imperative API (`bind:this`)

```svelte
<script>
  let tel: TelInput;
</script>

<TelInput bind:this={tel} />
<button onclick={() => tel.api.reset()}>Reset</button>
<button onclick={() => console.log(tel.api.checkValidity())}>Validate</button>
```

- `checkValidity(): { valid: boolean; error: ValidationError }` — triggers validation and returns the result.
- `reset(options?: { country?: boolean }): void` — clears value; restores country to `defaultCountry` (pass `{ country: true }` to reset country to `null`).

## Exports

```typescript
// Main package
import { TelInput, countries, parse, normalizeToE164, pickCountries } from 'svelte-tel-input';

// Types
import type { CountryCode, Country, DetailedValue, TelInputOptions, ValidationError, Props } from 'svelte-tel-input/types';

// Schema validator (Zod / Valibot / Yup)
import { validateTelInput } from 'svelte-tel-input/validators';
```

## `DetailedValue` shape

```typescript
interface DetailedValue {
  e164: string | null;               // "+36301234567"
  countryCode: CountryCode | null;   // "HU"
  countryCallingCode: string | null; // "36"
  nationalNumber: string | null;     // "301234567"
  formattedNumber: string | null;    // "+36 30 123 4567" (spaces only, no dashes/parens)
  formatInternational: string | null;// "+36 30 123 4567"
  formatNational: string | null;     // "(06 30) 123 4567"
  formatOriginal: string | null;     // "30 123 4567" (national significant digits, spaces)
  uri: string | null;                // "tel:+36301234567"
  isPossible: boolean;
  isPhoneValid: boolean;             // structurally valid (can be true even if country blocked)
  isValid: boolean;                  // overall — false if phone invalid OR country not allowed
  validationError: ValidationError;
}
```

## `ValidationError` values

`null` (valid) | `'TOO_SHORT'` | `'TOO_LONG'` | `'NOT_A_NUMBER'` | `'INVALID_COUNTRY'` | `'INVALID_LENGTH'` | `'INVALID'` | `'REQUIRED'` | `'COUNTRY_NOT_ALLOWED'`

## Utility functions

```typescript
// Parse any raw input
parse(raw: string, country?: CountryCode | null): Partial<DetailedValue>

// Normalize to E.164 or null
normalizeToE164(raw: string, country?: CountryCode | null): string | null
normalizeToE164('+36 30 123 4567')  // '+36301234567'
normalizeToE164('301234567', 'HU')  // '+36301234567'
normalizeToE164('not a phone')      // null

// Filtered country list in specified order
pickCountries(codes: CountryCode[]): Country[]
```

## Schema validation

```typescript
import { validateTelInput } from 'svelte-tel-input/validators';
// Returns null when valid, or a ValidationError string when not.

// Zod
const schema = z.object({
  phone: z.string().superRefine((val, ctx) => {
    const error = validateTelInput(val, { required: true });
    if (error) ctx.addIssue({ code: 'custom', message: error });
  })
});

// validateTelInput options
validateTelInput(value, {
  required?: boolean,
  allowedCountries?: CountryCode[],
  country?: CountryCode | null,   // hint for numbers without '+'
})
```

## Common patterns

### Country selector + phone input

```svelte
<script lang="ts">
  import { TelInput, countries } from 'svelte-tel-input';
  import type { CountryCode } from 'svelte-tel-input/types';

  let country: CountryCode | null = $state('US');
  let value = $state('');
  let valid = $state(true);
</script>

<select bind:value={country}>
  {#each countries as c (c.id)}
    <option value={c.iso2}>{c.name} (+{c.dialCode})</option>
  {/each}
</select>

<TelInput bind:country bind:value bind:valid required />
{#if !valid}<p role="alert">Invalid phone number</p>{/if}
```

### National format display

```svelte
<!-- Shows "215 456 7890" in the input; value stays "+12154567890" -->
<TelInput bind:value bind:country initialFormat="national" />
```

### Restricted countries

```svelte
<TelInput
  bind:value bind:valid bind:validationError
  options={{ allowedCountries: ['US', 'CA', 'GB'] }}
/>
<!-- validationError = 'COUNTRY_NOT_ALLOWED' for any other country -->
```

### Validate on blur only

```svelte
<TelInput bind:value bind:valid options={{ validateOn: 'blur' }} />
```

### Lock country

```svelte
<TelInput bind:country bind:value options={{ lockCountry: true }} />
<!-- Typing +44 will NOT switch country away from the pre-set value -->
```

### Reset and check validity imperatively

```svelte
<script>
  let tel: TelInput;
</script>

<TelInput bind:this={tel} bind:value defaultCountry="US" required />
<button onclick={() => tel.api.reset()}>Clear</button>
<button onclick={() => {
  const { valid, error } = tel.api.checkValidity();
  if (!valid) alert(error);
}}>Submit</button>
```

### Event-driven (no bindings)

```svelte
<TelInput
  onValueChange={(v, details) => console.log(v, details)}
  onCountryChange={(c) => console.log(c)}
  onValidityChange={(ok, err) => console.log(ok, err)}
/>
```
